claim: Trafilatura achieves F1=0.958 in the ScrapingHub article-extraction benchmark, the highest among Python open-source static extractors
Mozilla Readability achieves the highest median F1 (0.970) on article-type content in the SIGIR 2023 benchmark, outperforming trafilatura on clean article pages
Trafilatura has the best overall mean F1 (0.883) across 14 tools and 8 datasets in SIGIR 2023, generalizing better than Readability across diverse content types
Trafilatura v2.0.0 was released December 2024 and is actively maintained
Trafilatura has a known code block whitespace preservation issue (GitHub issue #553), making it imperfect for documentation sites
Turndown + GFM plugin has 2.5M weekly npm downloads and is the recommended HTML-to-Markdown conversion library for structured content
The recommended two-step extraction pipeline is: boilerplate removal (trafilatura) then HTML-to-Markdown conversion (Turndown+GFM)
Crawl4AI is licensed Apache-2.0, not MIT as some sources claim
Crawl4AI has 50k+ GitHub stars as of research date and provides Playwright-based rendering with a Docker REST API
Jina Reader was acquired by Elastic in October 2025, making its long-term free availability uncertain
Firecrawl credit stacking: Enhanced Mode (+4) + JSON extraction (+4) + base (1) = 9 credits per page, making heavy feature use expensive
Framework fingerprints alone are insufficient for JS detection — Next.js SSR pages have __NEXT_DATA__ but do not require JS rendering
A three-phase JS detection escalation algorithm (framework detection → content absence check → dual-run comparison) correctly identifies JS-rendering necessity
njsparser can extract structured data from React Server Component (RSC) flight payloads without full rendering
Playwright MCP provides 26 tools across 7 categories; browser_snapshot (accessibility tree) is the primary extraction mode, not screenshot
Playwright MCP costs approximately 114k tokens per session vs 27k for CLI, making it expensive at extraction scale
storage-state.json pattern allows Playwright MCP to persist authentication state across sessions
robots-parser v3.0.1 is the recommended Node.js library for robots.txt parsing, implementing RFC 9309 (2022 IETF standard)
Anthropic operates three user agents (ClaudeBot, Claude-User, Claude-SearchBot) that all respect robots.txt
OpenAI's ChatGPT-User removed robots.txt compliance in December 2025 — the first major AI lab to abandon voluntary compliance
hiQ v. LinkedIn: CFAA does not prohibit scraping publicly accessible data, but ToS violations still create civil liability; settlement was $500k (2022)
France CNIL fined KASPR €240k for scraping LinkedIn data (2024) under GDPR — public availability does not remove data protection obligations
llms.txt is an emerging standard (not official) for sites to declare AI training/scraping preferences; originated from fast.ai (Jeremy Howard)
Pre-flight compliance gate should have three tiers: hard-block (stop entirely), warn (require user acknowledgment), informational (log and proceed)
The Creator View should have 7 sections: Relevant To Your Work, What This Site Understands, Voice/POV, Where Your Approach Differs, The Challenge, The Warning (optional), Knowledge Candidates
Voice and Editorial POV is a new section for website-analysis (not present in repo-analysis) because website POV determines how claims should be synthesized
The Warning section is optional and should only appear when a genuine risk exists — not every site has a Warning
13 value axes structure the website Creator View, including content depth, editorial stance, link graph as knowledge map, and visual design philosophy
CRAAP framework (Currency, Relevance, Authority, Accuracy, Purpose) is the most widely used academic source evaluation framework and should be adapted for web
Kissane's 5 content quality dimensions (Appropriate, Useful, Clear, Consistent, Concise) complement CRAAP for editorial quality assessment
E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) is Google's internal quality framework applicable as creator-facing quality signal
Creator context (CLAUDE.md, SESSION_CONTEXT.md) should be injected at skill invocation to make the Creator View project-specific and relevant
Gemini Deep Research accepts custom format instructions, validating the approach of structured, instructable output for AI research tools

Site type taxonomy has 15 types: Blog, Documentation, SPA/App, E-commerce, Curated List, Forum/Community, News/Media, API Docs, Government, Academic, Portfolio, Landing Page, Registry/Directory, Tool/Utility, Social Media
Four-layer auto-classification uses JSON-LD @type (0.40), meta generator (0.30), URL/OG type (0.20), DOM structure (0.10) with 0.60 as the definitive classification threshold
Wappalyzer has 7,200+ CMS and technology detection signatures that can be bundled for offline use
AMP tags (link rel=amphtml or html amp) are a reliable indicator of news/media content type
Sitemap index structure (multiple sitemaps) indicates a large site with 10k+ pages without requiring crawling
Open Graph metadata is present on 64% of web pages (Web Almanac 2024, n=17M sites)
JSON-LD is present on 41% of web pages; 89.4% of schema.org implementations use JSON-LD format
All metadata (Open Graph, JSON-LD, Twitter Card, HTML meta) is extractable from static HTML without JS rendering
Cheerio is the recommended metadata extraction library at 12ms per file with best Node.js developer experience
sameAs in JSON-LD links a site/organization to Wikipedia, Wikidata, LinkedIn, and other canonical identities for entity disambiguation
Link scoring uses 7 components: context relevance/TF-IDF (0.25), anchor quality (0.20), position/semantic container (0.15), URL pattern (0.15), link type (0.10), novelty (0.10), alive check (0.05)
Semantic container scoring: article content (+1.0), main (+0.8), section (+0.5), aside (+0.1); nav/header/footer should be discarded as boilerplate
Link type taxonomy: Tutorial/Reference/Academic Paper/Tool (HIGH +0.3), Article/Discussion/Video (MEDIUM 0.0), Social/Marketing/Press Release (LOW -0.2)
Target pipeline time is under 5 seconds per page: fetch (1-2s), parse (50ms), pre-filter (100ms), score (200ms), alive check (1-2s parallel)
After pre-filtering for link density, typical pages yield 5-20 scoreable external content links
jusText link density threshold is 0.20 per-block; Readability threshold is 0.25-0.50 per-element — these use different measurement units and are not directly comparable
Web Almanac 2025: median page has 6 links, 90th percentile has 25 links — the 40-link threshold for expedition trigger correctly targets the top 10% of pages
External domain ratio is the best discriminator between content-rich pages and link farms/directories
LLM-based page classification achieves F1=0.894 for link density classification tasks
Awesome lists (curated link collections) have 800-900 links per page on average
8 freshness detection methods ordered by reliability: HTTP Last-Modified, ETag, HTML date meta, JSON-LD dateModified, visible dates, Git commit date, sitemap lastmod, copyright year
Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level is the recommended default readability formula; SMOG Grade is preferred for healthcare/medical content
Heading hierarchy is the primary information architecture signal: H1 count, H2-H6 depth, heading text quality, and heading structure as content organization proxy
Lexical density (unique words / total words) greater than 50% is a quality proxy that helps identify high-information-density content
Factual density of 2-3 facts per 100 words has been proposed as a quality metric but comes from a single source and has not been independently validated
11 absence pattern types: DEAD_BLOG, VENDOR_BROCHURE, SPA_SHELL, PAYWALLED_HARD, PAYWALLED_SOFT, CAPTIVE_JS, AGGREGATOR, LINK_FARM, GENERATED_CONTENT, CURATED_LIST_WEB, REGISTRY
IndieWeb webring membership is a positive quality signal for long-tail, high-value sites — these are human-curated and community-vetted
EU AI Act requires watermarking of AI-generated content above a threshold in EU jurisdictions (March 2025)
Google's internal contentEffort metric estimates effort invested in content creation — effort proxies (original examples, data, diagrams) correlate with quality
NavBoost creates a rich-get-richer feedback loop: high-traffic sites get more search visibility which increases traffic, systematically underrepresenting long-tail quality content
Four cross-site synthesis paradigms: thematic (primary/default), narrative, matrix, meta-pattern — thematic synthesis should be the default for open-ended creator research
Source weighting for cross-site synthesis: T1 original research (3x), T2 expert synthesis (2x), T3 aggregation (1x), T4 secondary aggregation (0.5x)
Optimal cross-site synthesis scale is 5-12 sites; below 5 is insufficient for triangulation; above 12 shows diminishing returns without systematic framework
Thematic saturation stopping rule: 3 consecutive sites yielding no new themes indicates synthesis is complete
Parallel analysis (analyze all sites independently before synthesis) prevents anchoring bias in cross-site synthesis
Google AI Overviews decomposes queries into 8-12 sub-queries for broad coverage with cross-variant verification
ResearchRabbit and Exa findSimilar are the closest analogs to Expedition Mode — no existing tool does site-centric, HITL multi-hop navigation from within a developer workflow
Expedition UX should present 3-5 options per step as a ranked list (not cards), always including 1 wildcard option using epsilon-greedy (4 high-relevance + 1 wildcard per step)
Perplexity related questions achieve 40% interaction rate vs. Google PAA 3% — framing as recommendations (not suggestions) drives engagement 13x higher
Default expedition budget: depth_max=3 hops, pages_max=15, ~2500 tokens/page, 10-15 seconds wall-clock per page
Academic/curated domains (arXiv, GitHub Awesome lists, Wikipedia) may viably support 5+ expedition hops — thematic saturation is a better stopping rule than fixed depth
Expedition state uses three-file pattern: expedition.meta.json (session metadata), expedition.snap.json (current tree snapshot), expedition.jsonl (append-only event log)
Flat array with parent pointers is the correct tree representation for expedition state — compatible with append-only JSONL and efficient for O(n) traversal at small n
Resume protocol: detect expedition-*.meta.json, read snap.json, replay JSONL events after last snapshot, reconstruct tree, present path summary before proceeding
Chromium stores browser history as a flat list not a tree — this validates the flat JSONL + parent pointer approach for expedition state
Plan-MCTS (February 2026) applies Monte Carlo Tree Search to LLM planning — potentially applicable to automated expedition navigation in future versions
V1 storage is zero-dependency enhanced markdown vault (.research/) with YAML frontmatter for Obsidian/Dataview compatibility
SQLite + markdown hybrid is the recommended v2 storage architecture for cross-session retrieval and structured queries
NotebookLM has no public API for consumer users — only enterprise REST API in alpha — making it unavailable for v1 skill integration
MCPVault (filesystem-based, no Obsidian required) is the recommended MCP server for vault storage integration
QMD MCP provides hybrid BM25+vector search over .research/ without external services — recommended for searching accumulated site analyses
Anytype now has an official MCP server (@anyproto/anytype-mcp), enabling integration with privacy-first knowledge management for creators using Anytype
Raindrop.io now has an MCP server, enabling bridging from URL bookmarks directly to site analyses
Karpathy multi-resolution pattern for storage: L0 (200 tokens, identity), L1 (1-2K, index), L2 (search results), L3 (full content) — never load L3 when L1 suffices
research-index.jsonl needs URL, domain, siteType, and techStack fields added to support website-analysis alongside existing repo-analysis entries
URL-to-slug algorithm: lowercase, hyphens for non-alphanumeric, double-hyphens for path separators, 80-char max, SHA-256(6) hash suffix for disambiguation, Windows MAX_PATH compliant

Schema parity with repo-analysis requires 6 shared artifacts (analysis.json, findings.jsonl, value-map.json with extensions) and 3 new website-only artifacts (tables.json, sitemap.json, expedition.jsonl)
Website-analysis adds links.json, assets.json, and meta.json as new artifacts not present in repo-analysis
/website-analysis fills a genuine market gap: all existing AI analysis tools are query-centric or crawl-centric; none are site-centric (analyzing a site as an artifact for creator use)

Perplexity Deep Research uses a 6-stage pipeline: query interpretation, multi-step retrieval (20-50 queries, 200+ pages), hybrid retrieval (BM25+embedding), multi-layer ML reranking, structured citation assembly, LLM synthesis
Perplexity's key insight: research depth is a function of query count and synthesis orchestration, not raw model capability — citations are structurally assigned before LLM generation
Exa builds search on neural next-link prediction: predicting what a knowledgeable person would link to next, using custom transformer models and a Rust vector DB
Exa findSimilar primitive could power expedition link recommendations by returning semantically similar pages given a current node URL
Google AI Overviews gives a 317% multimodal citation bonus to pages with images, charts, or video alongside text
Tavily has SOC 2 Type II certification and 99.99% SLA — purpose-built agent infrastructure with Extract, Crawl, and Research APIs as separate concerns
Diffbot Knowledge Graph contains 10 billion entities and costs $299+/month — enterprise-grade entity disambiguation at scale
Crawl4AI provides 3-layer extraction: LLM-free CSS/XPath patterns (fastest), cosine similarity ranking (intermediate), optional LLM extraction (highest quality)
Engineer View should focus on creator-relevant technical dimensions (freshness, trust, readability, IA) rather than full technical audits (accessibility, performance, SEO)
HITL (human-in-the-loop) satisficing research shows users accept good-enough choices rather than optimize — validates 3-5 expedition options per step (not more)
Four analysis modes in increasing cost: Quick Scan (static + Cheerio), Page Analysis (+ JS detection), Site Crawl (× N pages + link scoring), Expedition (+ HITL navigation + cross-site synthesis)
The skill should be JASON-OS portable: no project-specific logic in the core, creator context injected at runtime from CLAUDE.md/SESSION_CONTEXT.md
Firecrawl has 105k GitHub stars, offers AGPL cloud + Docker deployment, and supports 9 credits per page with Enhanced+JSON stacking
HITL browser agent research: users satisfice (accept good-enough) rather than optimize navigation choices, validating limited options per expedition step
Anti-design trend in 2025: sites with aggressive minimalism and stripped metadata may deliberately obscure editorial stance
First-hand experience language (I tested, we built, in my experience) is the primary positive signal for authentic voice vs. aggregated or generated content
Wappalyzer open-source patterns JSON can be bundled with the skill for offline CMS detection without external API calls
Sitemap lastmod values are often inaccurate — use HTTP Last-Modified headers for freshness instead of sitemap lastmod
Session logs as implicit knowledge base: Claude Code conversation logs contain structured data that could be retroactively indexed by the skill
Knowledge graph MCP enables relationship queries at scale (entity + observations + relations) for v2+ storage architecture
Expedition JSONL event types: node_visit, snapshot, budget_warning, expedition_paused, expedition_resumed, expedition_complete
High-link-density detection threshold: pages with more than 40 unique external links should trigger a cross-site synthesis suggestion
PRISMA-trAIce is an emerging framework for AI-assisted systematic reviews in cross-site synthesis contexts
Exa 2.0 embedding model was trained on a 144x H200 GPU cluster for over a month, making findSimilar a highly valuable free primitive for expedition navigation
Trafilatura's Rust port reaches F1=0.970 and Go port reaches F1=0.960 — the algorithm quality rather than language explains the performance advantage
Trafilatura provides SimHash-based deduplication built-in, eliminating need for separate content deduplication implementation
YAML frontmatter schema for site analysis markdown files enables Obsidian/Dataview compatibility and cross-tool portability
4-phase export adapter pattern allows site analysis artifacts to be exported to different knowledge management systems without re-analysis
InfraNodus provides knowledge graph analysis for detecting themes and gaps in text corpora — applicable to expedition synthesis output
Rosenfeld/Morville IA systems (organization, labeling, navigation, search) provide a framework for assessing website information architecture quality
Crawlee dual-run comparison (cosine similarity > 0.85 means static extraction sufficient) is a reliable automated test for JS rendering necessity
Exa Deep mode has 3.5 second P50 latency for complete content retrieval with semantic search — viable for expedition navigation with budget awareness
BM25 + dense embedding hybrid retrieval is the 2024-2025 state of the art for link ranking and content retrieval systems
Uniform paragraph length (±20% variance), hedging without specific detail, and zero external links in long-form content are suspicion signals for AI-generated content
The skill should expose only a minimal Playwright tool surface (navigate, snapshot, extract) and handle the 26 underlying Playwright MCP tools internally
